chemistry.bond_formed: C-C bond between phthalimide and arylmethyl group
chemistry.scope_summary: Various N-(2-bromoethyl) and N-(3-bromopropyl)phthalimides with different arylacetates (H, 4-MeO, 4-Me, 4-F substituents), yielding products in 80-95% under flow conditions

optimization_table.key_findings: Acetonitrile was found to be the most suitable solvent as it gave acceptable to good conversions for all three reaction steps without the formation of noticeable by-products

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: wrapped around Pyrex body with single 8 W UVB fluorescence tube at its center

figures.caption: Scheme 1.  Examples of bioactive 3-arylmethylene-2,3-dihydro-1 H -isoindolin-1-ones.  | Scheme 1.  Examples of bioactive 3-arylmethylene-2,3-dihydro-1 H -isoindolin-1-ones.

figures.key_information: Laboratory setup showing multiple components labeled A through E and F1 through F3, with gas lines G1 and G2. Component A appears to be a large black instrument, B and other components appear to be various analytical or processing equipment arranged on a laboratory bench.
figures.caption: Figure 1.  ( a ) Flow scheme; ( b ) Actual in-house multi-step flow reactor module (A: photo reactor, B:  ultrasonic reactor, C: ion exchange resin cartridge, D: thermal reactor, E: product collection flask, F1– 3: syringe pumps, G1/2: T-junctions).  | Figure 1.  ( a ) Flow scheme; ( b ) Actual in-house multi-step flow reactor module (A: photo reactor, B: ultrasonic reactor, C: ion exchange resin cartridge, D: thermal reactor, E: product collection flask, F1–3: syringe pumps, G1 / 2: T-junctions).

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Traditional batch processes for photodecarboxylative benzylation require long reaction times and multiple solvents, resulting in lower yields
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous flow approach provides superior yields, significantly shorter reaction times, and uses single solvent system
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 540 min to 78 min, yield increased from 45% to 77%, simplified solvent system to acetonitrile
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Core photodecarboxylative benzylation chemistry, substrate scope, UVB light irradiation
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow reduces exposure to hazardous reagents and provides better control over reaction conditions

figure_descriptions: Multi-step flow reactor module showing photoreactor, ultrasonic reactor, ion exchange resin cartridge, thermal reactor, and product collection with syringe pumps and T-junctions

notes: Well-documented batch-to-flow translation with clear quantitative improvements. Figure 1 confirms multi-step reactor setup with photoreactor as first component. Some concentration and scale data missing but core performance metrics are complete.
